notes: Capability candidates derived from the architecture pattern report and violation register only. The full module/component inventory packs were not present in this stage's input context, so several supporting_modules entries reference project/folder names rather than MOD-xxx IDs. Treat all entries as CANDIDATES pending architect review.

capabilities.name: Catalog Management
capabilities.description: Maintains the product catalog: items, brands, and types, including specifications used to query catalog data.

evidence.reason: Core catalog entity referenced in architecture-pattern-report.md
API endpoint surface for catalog item management, referenced in ARCH-VIOL-003

open_questions: Full set of catalog endpoints (list/update/delete) and Brand/Type entities were not enumerated in the provided evidence packs; confirm against component inventory.

capabilities.name: Shopping Basket Management
capabilities.description: Manages a buyer's in-progress basket/cart, including basket items and basket-related queries (e.g. basket with items).

evidence.reason: Aggregate root referenced in architecture-pattern-report.md as implementing IAggregateRoot

open_questions: Whether BasketService contains business rules (rich domain) or is a thin pass-through is unresolved per architecture-pattern-report.md competing pattern #1.

capabilities.name: Order Management
capabilities.description: Manages order placement and order history, including a denormalized snapshot of catalog item details at the time of order.

evidence.reason: Value object capturing catalog item snapshot at order time (ARCH-VIOL-001)

open_questions: Confirm whether the CatalogItemOrdered snapshot is an intentional anti-corruption boundary or accidental coupling (see ARCH-VIOL-001).

capabilities.name: Buyer / Customer Profile Management
capabilities.description: Represents the buyer/customer placing orders, including address information used during checkout.
open_questions: No direct evidence of Buyer-related endpoints or services was included in this stage's input; confirm against component inventory.

capabilities.name: Catalog Administration
capabilities.description: Administrative create/edit/manage operations for catalog items. Currently appears to be implemented twice across two separate front-ends.

evidence.reason: Razor Pages admin catalog edit surface (ARCH-VIOL-002)
Blazor admin catalog edit surface for the same entity (ARCH-VIOL-002)

open_questions: Determine canonical Admin surface (Razor Pages vs Blazor) before forward engineering an 'Admin' module/service. See ARCH-VIOL-002.

capabilities.name: Storefront Web Experience
capabilities.description: Customer-facing MVC/Razor Pages presentation layer over the shared ApplicationCore domain.

evidence.reason: Referenced as one of two presentation/API projects depending on ApplicationCore and Infrastructure, per architecture-pattern-report.md

open_questions: No component-level evidence for Web pages beyond the Admin pages cited in ARCH-VIOL-002 was provided in this stage's input.

capabilities.name: Public API / Integration Layer
capabilities.description: Ardalis.ApiEndpoints-based minimal API surface exposing catalog (and likely other) operations to external/admin clients.

evidence.reason: Project dependency on Infrastructure, per system-inventory-pack reference in architecture-pattern-report.md

open_questions: Full endpoint inventory for PublicApi (orders, baskets, buyers) not present in provided evidence.

capabilities.name: Admin Application Experience
capabilities.description: Blazor WASM/Server admin application that consumes PublicApi endpoints rather than ApplicationCore directly.

evidence.reason: Decorator service wrapping PublicApi CatalogItemEndpoints calls (ARCH-VIOL-003)

open_questions: Coupling to PublicApi contract shapes is a forward-engineering risk; see ARCH-VIOL-003 and api-contract-preservation-map.json.
